    morocco played like they wanted it more, well anyway extremely good defense and decisions by the fullbacks, decisive tackling by the centrebacks, this is what you have to do, you gotta wonder why you actually dont see this level of aggression in other top level UCL or WC games. my point about the quality of attackers for spain proved true, i mean olmo was alright but asensio, morata, sarabia... useless. also my point about the passing but not actually doing anything. and for once that zoltan guy is correct about the long shots, why did spain keep on passing to the wings when the attacker was in enough space in the center just outside the box to shoot?

  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    Great teams are those who can beat an opponent even when their opponent plays well defensively. That's because great teams either CREATE SHOOTING CHANCES that are impossible to defend (quick one touch passing game and immediate shots from a shooting range), or CREATE SET PIECE OPPORTUNITIES that are well rehearsed and surprising enough for the defence to be at a loss against those set pieces. Spain failed on both of those fronts because of their coach. I am sorry to say, but at this level you must know the aforementioned simple things to work with a talented team such as that of Spain. P.S. I also hope that Spain's disappointing stats - 926 completed passes out of 1019 attempted passes (91% precision) against a mere 1 shot on target - are convincing enough to finally discard the defensive minded coaches who think that possession based football is the name of the game. It is definitely not!

    3 years ago
     +1 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us

  • [ - ] neumi17 - member since 06-Apr-13

    Spain managed ONE shot on goal in 127 minutes + missed all their penalties. You can't get kicked out more deserved.

    3 years ago
     +8 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    Morocco won the strategic part of this game: they were the weaker team offensively, so they had confidence in their defence, which delivered in the end. Also, as far as logic is concerned, it made sense to carry the game to penalty kicks, as Morocco had the far better goalkeeper of the two teams, in Bono. Louis Enrique didn't have full confidence in his team's offensive capabilities and visibly they didn't practice shooting from a distance. In fact, Spain might not even has long range shots in its offensive arsenal, which means that they only rely on the errors committed by their opponents. Unfortunately for them, Morocco's defence was confident enough not to make any mistakes.

    3 years ago
     +5 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us

  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    Confidence in your strength and logic: these are the two crucial items for a winning strategy. Last night the Japanese had confidence in their strength - defence - but were totally illogical in carrying the game into penalties where they got exposed for their weakness in executing penalties. They should have forced a win in regular time. Today both teams have offensive strengths, yet they still confide in their defensive abilities. Playing safe and lack of long range shots shows a lack of confidence from both sides. That's why this game will also go to penalties. P.S. I don't even see well rehearsed set pieces that could save your ass when you are clueless like today.

    3 years ago
      Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
  • [ - ] DM3T - member since 21-May-22

    I would love to see another penalty shootout.

    3 years ago
     +1 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    Finally, both teams have sped up their game and also apply pressure to force errors. But they still don't shoot from a distance: not good enough!

    3 years ago
     +1 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    "Morocco defence is hard to break down....blah...blah...blah.....praise the TSN invited ex player commentators." Well, if you play old boys style slow football like Spain, then it is hard because you give the defence time to shot down the passing channels. Otherwise, if you speed up your passing game by enforcing one touch football, then there's no team who could stop you from scoring (e.g. last night Brazil finally gave up on his previous defensive mindset).

    3 years ago
     +1 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us

  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    @FootballFanatics ...because they could not pay me enough! LOL ....and because they don't need quality: can't you see what kind of coaches are there virtually everywhere? Clueless Portugues and Brazilian coaches with totally defensive mindset that contaminates even the minds of other coaches who mistakenly think that the wise thing to do is to play safely (aka. out of fear). The time of real coaches like Jupp Heinkess or Johann Cruyff is over - Cruyff even said that football is a very simple game, but it is very difficult to play it the simple way (probably because of the fear factor).

  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    Both teams with strong defences and yet they don't shoot from a distance. Total nonsense! You don't need a coach to play this stupid "game plan". As a player I just go out and pass the ball around in the safest manner possible (aka. short range passing without risk, but also without any promise offensively). To score you need to take risks: none of these teams are taking any risk. What's more, they don't even excel at set pieces, so I think it will be a low scoring game (even 0-0 is possible). Remember: Marocco has already played 0-0 against a team very similar to Spain: Croatia.

    3 years ago
      Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
  • [ - ] Ewerfekt - member since 16-Oct-18

    @Zoltan I am also wondering why we don't see more long shots in general on this WC. There is crazy amount of good low block teams yet everybody is trying to walk into goal. Maybe teams are scared of counters from lost balls, yet as you say some risk must be made if you want to win,

    3 years ago
     +2 Reply Report Sign in or join us to rate comments! Sign In || Join Us
  • [ - ] Zoltan - member since 15-Feb-20

    @Ewerfekt Actually, the least risk for a counterattack is when you shoot form long range. No, I think that much acclaimed Spanish possession game (the tiki-taka) has became so pervasive in modern football, that coaches have forgotten about the essence of game, which is to score goals. And scoring goals can NOT only happen one way - from the inside of the box - in fact against a team parking the bus such as Morocco, trying to score from within the box is the least optimal solution. That's why Spain lost today and that's why I don't understand what Luis Enrique was doing today on the bench!

  • [ - ] Icarax - member since 27-Sep-17

    for once im gonna go for the underdogs (also cuz now ive actually watched the teams). spain's stubbornness to play possession gives the impression that they dominate but honestly as long as they dont find openings its totally fruitless, especially in a cup tournament teams can beat them in the small details or counterattacks as japan did and theyre out. they are more direct and therefore dangerous this year than in the previous 2 editions, not sure they can win the world cup with players like olmo, asensio, morata and ferran as regulars, but if they do itll be after getting low-scoring draws. i think a great lively underdog like morocco can get them out already due to these things (as opposed to another european giant with their boring carefulness also trying to outplay spain)
